issue_text:
Title: Agent host session subscriptions are never released after the chat is closed
Body: ## Problem

Once the user opens an agent host session in the chat panel, the `IAgentSubscription<ISessionState>` acquired by [`AgentHostSessionHandler`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts) is never released — it stays open for the lifetime of the window (or until the chat content provider unregisters).

Trace:

1. `provideChatSessionContent` calls `_ensureSessionSubscription(resolvedSession)` (around [agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L450](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L450)) and stores the released ref in the `AgentHostChatSession`'s onDispose callback ([line ~526](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L526)).
2. The `IChatSession` is cached by `ChatSessionsService._sessions` in [`getOrCreateChatSession`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatSessions/chatSessions.contribution.ts#L1012).
3. The cache entry is only disposed when (a) the content provider unregisters, (b) a contribution's when-clause turns it off, or (c) the session itself fires `onWillDispose`. **There is no editor-close hook.**

So closing the chat editor doesn't dispose the `IChatSession`, which means `_releaseSessionSubscription` is never called, which means the connection-layer refcount never reaches zero, which means we keep receiving full `ISessionState` delta envelopes (turns, tool calls, queued messages, etc.) for that session for the rest of the window's life.

## Scope

This is **not** a "subscribe to all running sessions on startup" problem — the session list view is fed by `listSessions()` summaries plus connection-level `SessionAdded` / `SessionRemoved` notifications, and never opens per-session subscriptions on its own. Only the chat content path (and now the running-session config picker provider) takes per-session subscriptions.

The cost grows linearly with the number of sessions the user has clicked into during a window's lifetime.

## Expected behavior

Drop the subscription when the chat editor closes, **but** keep it alive while the session is still running so background updates (tool-complete, turn-complete, status changes, auto-rename, etc.) continue to flow for sessions the user might come back to.

Concretely:

- **Chat editor closes** → if the session is in a terminal state, release immediately. Otherwise, arm a one-shot listener that releases on the next terminal-state transition.
- **Session reaches terminal state** → if the chat editor is already closed, release.

## Open question

What counts as "terminal"? `Done` / archived seems right. `Idle` is too aggressive — sessions sit `Idle` between turns and the user might be coming back to send another message in 30 seconds.

## Notes

- The connection-layer refcounting in `AgentSubscriptionManager` already supports this cleanly — this is purely a question of when the chat handler releases its ref.
- The `ChatSessionsService._sessions` cache lifecycle is a separate (related) question: today an `IChatSession` is created on first open and never invalidated when the editor closes. The fix here may need to either hook into editor-close at the `ChatSessionsService` level, or leave the `IChatSession` cached but have the handler scope the *subscription* lifetime more tightly than the `IChatSession` itself.

(Written by Copilot)

  "requirement": "Issue Title: Agent host session subscriptions are never released after the chat is closed\nIssue Body: ## Problem\n\nOnce the user opens an agent host session in the chat panel, the `IAgentSubscription<ISessionState>` acquired by [`AgentHostSessionHandler`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts) is never released — it stays open for the lifetime of the window (or until the chat content provider unregisters).\n\nTrace:\n\n1. `provideChatSessionContent` calls `_ensureSessionSubscription(resolvedSession)` (around [agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L450](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L450)) and stores the released ref in the `AgentHostChatSession`'s onDispose callback ([line ~526](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts#L526)).\n2. The `IChatSession` is cached by `ChatSessionsService._sessions` in [`getOrCreateChatSession`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/chatSessions/chatSessions.contribution.ts#L1012).\n3. The cache entry is only disposed when (a) the content provider unregisters, (b) a contribution's when-clause turns it off, or (c) the session itself fires `onWillDispose`. **There is no editor-close hook.**\n\nSo closing the chat editor doesn't dispose the `IChatSession`, which means `_releaseSessionSubscription` is never called, which means the connection-layer refcount never reaches zero, which means we keep receiving full `ISessionState` delta envelopes (turns, tool calls, queued messages, etc.) for that session for the rest of the window's life.\n\n## Scope\n\nThis is **not** a \"subscribe to all running sessions on startup\" problem — the session list view is fed by `listSessions()` summaries plus connection-level `SessionAdded` / `SessionRemoved` notifications, and never opens per-session subscriptions on its own. Only the chat content path (and now the running-session config picker provider) takes per-session subscriptions.\n\nThe cost grows linearly with the number of sessions the user has clicked into during a window's lifetime.\n\n## Expected behavior\n\nDrop the subscription when the chat editor closes, **but** keep it alive while the session is still running so background updates (tool-complete, turn-complete, status changes, auto-rename, etc.) continue to flow for sessions the user might come back to.\n\nConcretely:\n\n- **Chat editor closes** → if the session is in a terminal state, release immediately. Otherwise, arm a one-shot listener that releases on the next terminal-state transition.\n- **Session reaches terminal state** → if the chat editor is already closed, release.\n\n## Open question\n\nWhat counts as \"terminal\"? `Done` / archived seems right. `Idle` is too aggressive — sessions sit `Idle` between turns and the user might be coming back to send another message in 30 seconds.\n\n## Notes\n\n- The connection-layer refcounting in `AgentSubscriptionManager` already supports this cleanly — this is purely a question of when the chat handler releases its ref.\n- The `ChatSessionsService._sessions` cache lifecycle is a separate (related) question: today an `IChatSession` is created on first open and never invalidated when the editor closes. The fix here may need to either hook into editor-close at the `ChatSessionsService` level, or leave the `IChatSession` cached but have the handler scope the *subscription* lifetime more tightly than the `IChatSession` itself.\n\n(Written by Copilot)\n\nPlease generate tasks and acceptance criteria.",

issue_text: Title: Respect the user's preferred shell in agent host terminals Body: Local, remotes are more complicated than I thought, created separate follow up issues, references below in history. Today, host-managed terminals in the agent host (the PTY-backed `bash`/`powershell` tools in [`copilotShellTools.ts`](https://github.com/microsoft/vscode/blob/main/src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/copilotShellTools.ts)) hardcode the shell — on Windows that's always windows pwsh (pwsh 5) regardless of what the user has set up. > If I have PowerShell 7 installed I think agent host terminal would still go to windows powershell (pwsh5) Making the default better (well... some windows users are passionate git bash users all time) and also respecting user's preferred shell (such as the one defined in setting) would be great. We allow user to set per OS like: ` "terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.windows":` - **(a)** Inherit the existing integrated-terminal settings — `terminal.integrated.defaultProfile.*` - The agent host can't read workbench config directly?? because it's a separate Node process, but the workbench-side `NodeAgentHostStarter` already has `IConfigurationService` and forwards data to the child via env vars and the AHP root-config bag — same channel `customizations` uses today. So the workbench resolves the profile via `ITerminalProfileResolverService` and pushes the resolved path/args down on startup and on setting change. - **(b)** Add a dedicated `agentHost.defaultShell` setting. Simple and isolated, but creates a parallel surface users have to configure separately. - **(c)** Both — dedicated setting wins if explicitly set, otherwise inherit from `terminal.integrated.*`. Maybe we'd have dedicated setting for agent host terminal, but if the value is not set there we'll respect user's default terminal profile setting. Existing behavior on Copilot CLI on chat panel:

issue_text:
Title: AgentHost: Selection attachments drop selected text and range
Body: (Codex) 

## Problem

The Copilot CLI chat-panel path preserves editor selection context when sending a request: it forwards the selected file, selected text, and selection range.

The Agent Host path currently converts an implicit selection into a selection attachment, but only includes the file URI and display name. The selected text and range are dropped before the request reaches the Copilot SDK.

## Current Context

There are incoming agent host protocol changes for richer attachments. This issue should track the VS Code-side implementation work after those changes land, rather than extending the current older `file` / `directory` / `selection` attachment shape directly.

## Evidence

- `src/vs/workbench/contrib/chat/browser/agentSessions/agentHost/agentHostSessionHandler.ts`
  - `_convertVariablesToAttachments` handles implicit selections, but emits only `{ type, uri, displayName }` today.
- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/common/agentService.ts`
  - `IAgentAttachment` already has optional `text` and `selection` fields in the current VS Code-side interface.
- `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/copilot/copilotAgentSession.ts`
  - `CopilotAgentSession.send` already forwards `text` and `selection` to the public Copilot SDK if present.
- `extensions/copilot/src/extension/chatSessions/copilotcli/node/copilotcliPromptResolver.ts`
  - The existing chat-panel CLI path preserves richer selection context.

## Expected Behavior

When a user sends a Copilot CLI request through Agent Host with editor selection context, the Agent Host path should preserve:

- selected file URI/path
- selected text
- selection start/end line and character

This should match the current Copilot CLI chat-panel behavior and avoid losing important context after moving CLI integration to Agent Host.

## Likely Fix

After the richer attachment shape lands, wire editor selection attachments through the VS Code Agent Host path:

- Convert implicit editor selection variables into the richer selection attachment shape.
- Preserve selected text and selection range when converting chat variables in `AgentHostSessionHandler`.
- Preserve those fields through Agent Host side effects/state.
- Map the richer Agent Host selection attachment into the Copilot SDK selection attachment shape in `CopilotAgentSession.send`.

## Classification

Agent Host implementation gap. The public Copilot SDK already accepts selection `text` and `selection` fields; the remaining work is VS Code-side Agent Host wiring after the incoming agent host protocol changes land.

issue_text: Title: Allow Claude Agent to run through Anthropic subscription instead of Copilot quota Body: I would like VS Code to support using **Claude Agent (Preview)** directly through an **Anthropic / Claude subscription or Anthropic API credentials**, instead of only consuming the user’s **GitHub Copilot subscription**. This feature request comes from a real misunderstanding I had while using the feature. Since Claude Agent is described as using the official Anthropic agent harness / Claude Agent SDK, I initially assumed that the Claude usage would be tied to my existing Claude/Anthropic subscription, or at least that there would be a way to use it directly through Anthropic. The confusion mainly came from the agent/model selector, where seeing Claude Agent listed made me assume that it could be used through my Claude/Anthropic subscription. Because I misunderstood this billing model, I ended up spending around **$300 extra in requests in a single week**. This is not meant as a complaint, but as context for why making the billing/authentication model more explicit, or supporting direct Anthropic usage, would be valuable. Ideally, Claude Agent could offer a provider/authentication option such as: - Use via GitHub Copilot subscription - Use via Anthropic / Claude subscription - Use via Anthropic API key or Claude Code authentication This would allow users to choose explicitly how Claude Agent usage is authenticated and billed. If supporting direct Anthropic authentication inside the existing Claude Agent option is not desirable, I would also see it as a viable alternative to add a separate option to the current agent/model selector, clearly indicating that this version runs through the user’s Anthropic/Claude subscription instead of Copilot. For example, the selector could include two clearly separated entries: - Claude Agent — via Copilot subscription - Claude Agent — via Anthropic subscription This would make the billing model explicit and avoid confusion, while still preserving the current Copilot-based integration as the default or recommended option. From a user perspective, this seems potentially feasible, since the existing Claude extension for VS Code already allows users to work with Claude through their own Claude/Anthropic access. I understand there may be product, technical, or legal considerations on the VS Code/Copilot side, but having a similar option in the integrated chat/agent selector would be much more convenient. The main benefit would be that developers who already use Claude or Claude Code could use the full Claude Agent SDK experience inside VS Code without consuming Copilot quota or premium requests. It would also make the integration more flexible and clearer, especially for users who want to keep Copilot and Anthropic usage separate. Even if direct Anthropic authentication is not planned, it would be very helpful to make the billing/usage model more explicit in the UI, so users do not mistakenly assume that Claude Agent usage is covered by their Claude subscription.
